C.why: The text describes how the city is organized into parts.
C.evidence: The ancient centre of Rome is divided into 22 districts, the names of most dating back to Classical times, while surrounding it are 35 urban sectors that began to be officially absorbed into the municipality after 1911.

D.why: The text describes the weather in Rome.
D.evidence: Rome s hot, dry summer days, with high temperatures often above 24 C, are frequently cooled in the afternoons by a west wind that rises from the Tyrrhenian Sea.

E.why: The text talks about the main street in central Rome.
E.evidence: The main street in central Rome is Via del Corso, an important thoroughfare since Classical times, when it was the Via Flaminia, the road to the Adriatic.

F.why: The text talks about Rome's fountains and water.
F.evidence: Rome is as much a city of fountains as it is of churches or palaces, antiquities or urban problems.

G.why: The text talks about the hills on which Rome was built.
G.evidence: Rome was built on a defensible hill that dominated the last downstream, high-banked river crossing.

D.evidence: Sleep plays an essential role in your health and well-being.

E.why: This text tells how many hours people should sleep each night.
E.evidence: On average, adults should optimally receive between seven and nine hours of sleep each night, but those needs vary individually.

F.why: This text explains how not sleeping enough can lead to obesity.
F.evidence: A growing body of research suggests that there s a link between how much people sleep and how much they weigh.

G.why: This text shows how emotions like sadness affect sleep and vice versa.

D.why: The text describes how the tilt of Earth causes different seasons.

F.why: The text describes Antarctica's six-month day and six-month night.

A.why: The text gives short facts about Paris, like its location, size, and population.

B.why: The text explains the good things about living in Paris, like culture and business.

D.why: The text talks about Paris's climate, temperature, and changeable weather.

F.why: The text shows how the population of Paris grew from 600,000 to over 10 million.

82A876.why: The text says 'a few minor issues were catching me off balance' and describes small problems. tv shows and weather were expected, not confusing.

A45147.why: The author says 'I have found lots of pleasant surprises in the UK too – and so have many other international students I know.' This means positive surprises happen.
